Alerts & notifications
Critical — act now
DATP Public Comment reading room is live. The City of Colorado Springs Digital Accessibility Transition Plan (Draft 2026), with the full plan embedded as a PDF and twelve structured public comments residents can adapt and submit. Comment portal links live in the reading room.
Public comment · COS Digital Accessibility Transition Plan · May 2026
Open the DATP reading room →
BOCC petition — service plan transparency & algorithmic pricing disclosure. A general resident petition with four narrow asks within existing BOCC authority. Sign and send.
Civic action · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Open petition letter →
Veterans' petition to the EPC BOCC. Companion petition for veterans of any branch — service plan transparency and algorithmic pricing protections, framed around military housing harms.
Civic action · Veterans · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Open veterans' petition →
LDC update — public comment on service plan transparency. A 300-word comment ready to submit through the El Paso County Land Development Code update process.
Public comment · LDC update · El Paso County · May 2026
Open LDC comment →
Background brief — algorithmic rent pricing in El Paso County. Sourced reference for journalists, policy analysts, and the COS Chamber housing committee: federal antitrust action, local policy options, and the case for BOCC-level service plan transparency.
Reference · Housing & pricing · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Read background brief →
HOME-ARP comment window is live — closes May 26, 2026. COS is collecting public comment on the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment via an ArcGIS Survey123 form Esri's own ACR rates as "Partially Supports" on WCAG 2.1 A/AA. A WCAG 2.1 AA Drupal Webform replacement is ready to install — bilingual EN/ES, JSON webhook + staff email + native storage, inline accommodation intake.
24 CFR 91.105 · ADA Title II · May 4, 2026
Open the accessible HTML form preview → Open the HOME-ARP bundle README →
Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.
Annual adopted budget — C.R.S. 29-1-101 et seq · April 10, 2026
Open the search portal →
ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
Open district audit dashboard →
249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
See district audit →
WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
Platform audit · April 2026
Draft compliance letter →
High priority
All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
ADA document audit · April 2026
View ADA analysis →
0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
Governance gap · §32-1-202
See audit dashboard →
Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.
Governance gap · §32-1-106 · due July 31
See audit dashboard →
Informational
2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.
CORA tracker · April 2026
View tracker →
Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.
Compliance analysis · April 2026
Read analysis →
Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
New · April 2026
Find my jurisdiction →
CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open blotter dashboard →
Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
New · Special Districts · April 2026
Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open CBI form rebuild →
LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open ADAM dashboard →

County · CitizenConnect

ADA / WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit — Citizen Connect

citizenconnect.elpasoco.com  ·  Audited April 14, 2026  ·  WCAG 2.1 AA + axe-core 4.9.1

Automated scan (axe-core) + manual DOM inspection. Does not substitute a full manual screen-reader test.

Functional issues beyond WCAG

The ticket system loses residents' submissions

These problems don't violate accessibility rules, but they break the service for residents who rely on it. They're at least as urgent as the WCAG findings below.

  • The reply ticket does not include the original submission. When the county responds, the resident receives the reply alone — with no quoted copy of what they originally submitted, no address or date, and no thread. They're reading an answer with no way to recall the question, and no way to verify the reply actually addresses what they reported.
  • Previous email correspondence can disappear. Follow-up emails between a resident and the county are not reliably attached to the ticket thread. Residents lose the audit trail of what was communicated, and staff can't easily pick up a case handed off from another department.
  • Pothole submission is broken. The pothole-reporting form does not complete submission. Residents receive no confirmation, and no ticket appears in the system. The defect is silent — users believe their report was filed when it was not.
Recommended fix: Replace or supplement the automated ticket system with a monitored intake inbox staffed by a real person who routes each request to the right department. Email preserves the full thread automatically, gives residents a durable receipt, and re-introduces a human who can read the first sentence of a broken pothole report and still get it to Public Works.
1Serious
3Moderate
5Minor / Best practice
44Rules passing

Serious — fix before launch

Serious
Search input has no accessible label
The "Check Status" service-request search field (#sr_number) has no <label>, no aria-label, and no aria-labelledby. Only a placeholder is present, which disappears when the user starts typing and is never read reliably by screen readers.
Fix: Add <label for="sr_number">Service request number</label> — visually hide it with a .sr-only class if needed — or add aria-label="Service request number" directly on the input.
1.3.1 · 4.1.2

Moderate — fix soon

Moderate
Mobile menu toggle is keyboard-inaccessible
The hamburger menu uses a CSS checkbox-hack: a real <input type="checkbox" id="showmenu"> set to display:none. Because it is hidden via CSS, it is removed from the accessibility tree entirely. Keyboard and screen-reader users cannot open the mobile navigation.
Fix: Replace with a <button> that toggles the nav with JS. Apply aria-expanded and aria-controls to the button, and manage focus into the menu on open.
2.1.1 · 4.1.2
Moderate
Icon images have empty alt text with no decorative role
Four homepage icons (icon1–icon4.png) have alt="" but are not marked role="presentation" or aria-hidden="true", and none sit inside a link or labeled container that would provide context. If they convey service categories they need descriptive alt text; if they are purely decorative, they need explicit presentational role.
Fix: Add alt="Service requests" / etc. if meaningful, or role="presentation" if purely decorative. Also add aria-hidden="true" to the surrounding SVG icons.
1.1.1
Moderate
News article thumbnail images have empty alt text
Three news thumbnails (NR-WEB-IMAGE-TEMPLATE-6.png, Vote-NR-Image.png, NR-WEB-IMAGE-TEMPLATE-3-1.png) have alt="". They appear to be informative editorial photos that provide context for each article — making them non-decorative and requiring meaningful descriptions.
Fix: Add descriptive alt text reflecting the content of each image (e.g., alt="El Paso County Extension office building"), or alt="" with role="presentation" only if genuinely decorative.
1.1.1

Minor / best practice

Minor
Positive tabindex values disrupt tab order
The service-request input has tabindex="5" and the "Check Status" button has tabindex="6". Positive tabindex values pull elements to the front of the tab sequence regardless of DOM order, which can confuse keyboard users navigating the rest of the page.
Fix: Remove explicit tabindex values and rely on natural DOM order. Use CSS to reorder visual presentation if needed.
2.4.3
Minor
Multiple navigation landmarks have no distinguishing labels
The page contains several <nav> elements (main nav, footer links, etc.) with no aria-label or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users who browse by landmarks will hear repeated announcements of "navigation" with no way to differentiate them.
Fix: Add aria-label="Main", aria-label="Footer", etc. to each nav element.
2.4.1
Minor
article elements use inappropriate role="listitem"
Three news <article> elements in the "In the News" grid have role="listitem". The listitem role is not permitted on article elements per ARIA spec. This flag is marked "incomplete" by axe because these elements are currently hidden, but the pattern will cause issues when visible.
Fix: Remove the role="listitem" attribute from article elements. If you need list semantics, wrap items in a <ul> with <li> elements instead of articles.
4.1.2
Minor
Third-party iframe embeds need verification
Two embedded iframes (YouTube widget via woxo.tech, Twitter timeline) are present. The YouTube widget title reads "Youtube widgets for website" — generic. The Twitter iframe titled "Twitter settings iframe" is a hidden utility frame, but if the Twitter timeline widget itself is present it requires a meaningful title. Third-party widgets often have significant internal accessibility issues.
Fix: Give the YouTube embed a descriptive title (title="El Paso County video highlights"). Audit third-party widgets independently or replace with natively accessible alternatives.
4.1.2
Minor
Page title is not sufficiently descriptive
The page title is "El Paso County, Colorado" — identical to the root domain's title. In multi-tab workflows, users cannot distinguish this tab from others. A more descriptive title aids screen reader orientation and browser history scanning.
Fix: Change to something like "Citizen Connect — El Paso County, Colorado" to uniquely identify the portal.
2.4.2

Color contrast check (all computed)

Nav links — white on navy
rgb(255,255,255) on rgb(0,45,93)
13.7 : 1 ✓
Utility bar — white on black
rgb(255,255,255) on rgb(0,0,0)
21.0 : 1 ✓
Login Page link — light blue on maroon
rgb(191,215,237) on rgb(111,41,40)
7.0 : 1 ✓
Announcement bar — white on red
rgb(255,255,255) on rgb(232,28,28)
4.6 : 1 ✓
Check Status button — navy on light gray
rgb(0,45,93) on rgb(228,228,228)
10.8 : 1 ✓
Body text — dark on white
rgb(35,35,35) on rgb(255,255,255)
15.7 : 1 ✓

All measured contrast ratios pass WCAG 2.1 AA thresholds (4.5:1 normal text · 3:1 large text).

What's working well

  • ✓ Skip navigation link present (#content_start)
  • ✓ lang="en-US" set on <html>
  • ✓ No duplicate element IDs
  • ✓ Heading hierarchy logical (H1 → H2 → H3)
  • ✓ All 6 color pairs pass contrast AA
  • ✓ Main landmark present
  • ✓ Header / footer landmarks present
  • ✓ No autoplay audio or video detected
  • ✓ Form inputs have associated labels or titles
  • ✓ 44 / 44 axe-core rule categories pass

Recommended next step: Run a manual screen-reader walkthrough with NVDA + Firefox and VoiceOver + Safari, focusing on the mobile menu toggle, the service-request search form, and the social media embeds. Automated tools catch ~30–40% of real-world accessibility failures; the issues flagged above are the highest-confidence items from combined automated + DOM analysis.

Get email updates

We'll email you a plain-language summary with links to the source documents whenever this office posts new public-record material.

By subscribing you agree to receive emails about this topic. We'll send a confirmation link first. Unsubscribe any time. (consent v2026-04-30-v1)
